text: The surgeon has completely removed adhesions from the anterior abdominal wall involving the uterus and intestines on the side wall. Subsequently, the uterus is visibly removed from the surgical field.

The surgeon identifies a water-filled structure attached to the anterior abdominal wall, womb, and uterus. The structure is carefully dissected and cut from all directions to achieve complete liberation.

The surgeon identifies film adhesions in the abdominal cavity, cutting them from distant areas. A visible mass is located close to the front, and additional adhesions are being cut from the back of this mass.
The surgeon continues the operation, ensuring complete liberation of the adhesions. Blood vessels are meticulously cleaned and checked to confirm that they are fully closed.
The surgeon places a plastic bag inside another plastic bag and wraps it securely, ensuring containment within the surgical field.
